compounddef.innerclass: Dali::UniquePtr< Type, Deleter, typename EnableIf<!IsFunctionPointer< Deleter >::value >::type >

briefdescription.para: There is no state associated with this gesture. @SINCE_1_9.28.
The touched points on the screen have moved enough to be considered a gesture. @SINCE_1_9.28.
The gesture is continuing. @SINCE_1_9.28.
The user has lifted a finger or touched an additional point on the screen. @SINCE_1_9.28.
The gesture has been cancelled. @SINCE_1_9.28.
A gesture is possible. @SINCE_1_9.28.
Enumeration for state of the gesture. @SINCE_1_9.28.
invalid data
mouse
touch
Enumeration for gesture input source type. @SINCE_2_2.0.
invalid data

detaileddescription.para: Primary(Left) mouse button
Secondary(Right) mouse button
Center(Wheel) mouse button

briefdescription.para: Enumeration for data of gesture input source type. @SINCE_2_2.0.

detaileddescription.para: The AddOnLibrary type represents fully opaque object which hides the actual handle to the library and other related data.
These types are equivalent to those in the GLES2 API. Dali objects should only access GL indirectly, through the Context API.

briefdescription.para: The major version number of the Core library.
The minor version number of the Core library.
The micro version number of the Core library.
The date/time the Core library was built.

simplesect.para: Use of friend enables infix notation on free function, whilst retaining documentation inside the class scope. @SINCE_2_5.14
A new String containing the concatenation of lhs and rhs

detaileddescription.para:
Combine two bitfields
lhs
bitfield to or
rhs
bitfield to or
EnumType with both flags set

Combine two bitfields
lhs
bitfield to or
rhs
bitfield to or
reference to lhs with both flags set

Test two bitfields.
lhs
bitfield to AND
rhs
bitfield to AND
true if at least one flag is same in both

briefdescription.para: Create a hash code for a string.
Create a hash code for a string.
Create a hash code for 2 strings combined. Allows a hash to be calculated without concatenating the strings and allocating any memory.
Create a hash code for a string_view.
Create a hash code for a string_view.
Create a hash code for 2 string_views combined. Allows a hash to be calculated without concatenating the string_views and allocating any memory.
Create a hash code for a std::vector<std::uint8_t>
Create a hash code for a Dali::Vector<std::uint8_t>

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint8_t *const imagePixels, const Size &imageSize, uint8_t *const distanceMap, const Size &distanceMapSize, const float fieldRadius, const uint32_t fieldBorder, bool highQuality)
memberdef.name: GenerateDistanceFieldMap

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint8_t *const imagePixels, const Vector2 &imageSize, uint8_t *const distanceMap, const Vector2 &distanceMapSize, const uint32_t fieldBorder, const Vector2 &maxSize, bool highQuality=true)
memberdef.name: GenerateDistanceFieldMap

briefdescription.para: Generate a distance field map from a source image.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to a buffer containing the source image
The size, width and height, of the source image
A pointer to a buffer to receive the calculated distance field map. Note: This must not overlap with imagePixels for correct distance field map generation.
The size, width and height, of the distance field map
The amount of distance field cells to add around the data (for glow/shadow effects)
The image is scaled from this size to distanceMapSize
Set true to generate high quality distance fields

briefdescription.para: Creates a Property::Array from an initializer_list.

detaileddescription.para: This devel-api function allows convenient initialization of Property::Array using brace-enclosed initializer lists.

simplesect.para: A Property::Array containing the provided values

briefdescription.para: Creates a Property::Map from an initializer_list.

detaileddescription.para: This devel-api function allows convenient initialization of Property::Map using brace-enclosed initializer lists.

simplesect.para: A Property::Map containing the provided key-value pairs

briefdescription.para: Creates a Property::Value (as a Map) from an initializer_list.

detaileddescription.para: This devel-api function allows convenient initialization of Property::Value as a map using brace-enclosed initializer lists.

simplesect.para: A Property::Value containing a Property::Map with the provided key-value pairs

briefdescription.para: Helper function building the version number as 32-bit integer. The return value should be used to encode AddOnInfo::version field.
The DALi namespace. @SINCE_1_0.0.

detaileddescription.para: < for std::chrono::time_point
< for uint32_t, size_t
The class should implement Dali::ConnectionTrackerInterface, or inherit from Dali::ConnectionTracker.
< Dali::Min , Dali::Max
< for time(nullptr)
< Dali::Swap
< for std::move()
< for srand() and rand()

As the Implementation is bit complex because of Small Buffer Optimization (SBO) In the Property::Value class and Tagged Union implementation in the Impl class. Here is the brief description of the motivation and internal of this implementation.

The main motivation is to keep the value semantic without doing any heap allocation.
In the public class we keep a aligned buffer of 16byte instead of keeping the Pimpl pointer.
we create the Impl object inplace in that buffer to avoid the memory allocation.
we don't store the Impl* along with the SBO storage to save 8byte from the Property::Value object.
Every time we need to access Impl object we access it through Read() and Write() which retrieves the already constructed Impl object from the SBO storage.
with SBO , the move and assignment operator are tricky , so we use std::copy to move the object and update the moved from object to NONE to keep it in a safe state.
In the Impl class, we keep a Uninitialized Union and manage lifecycle of objects by doing construct/destroy manually.
We keep the Type information along with the Object to keep the size as 16byte (using common initial sequence(CIS) ).
This enforces automatic disconnection when an object is destroyed, so you don't have to manually disconnect from signals.
Alternatively, you can use a Dali::SlotDelegate if you don't want to inherit.
The top level DALi namespace
